questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Nitrogen dioxide reacts with ozone to form Dinitrogen Pentoxide and Oxygen. The reaction can be represented as.
$2 \mathrm{NO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_3(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})$
Experimentally the overall rate of the reaction was found to be first order in both $\mathrm{NO}_2$ as well as $\mathrm{O}_3$ i.e. rate $=\mathrm{K}\left[\mathrm{NO}_2\right]\left[\mathrm{O}_3\right]$

Mechanism I:
$\begin{gathered}
\mathrm{NO}_2+\mathrm{O}_3 \rightarrow \mathrm{NO}_3+\mathrm{O}_2 \text { Slow } \\
\mathrm{NO}_3+\mathrm{NO}_2 \rightarrow \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5 \text { Fast }
\end{gathered}$
Mechanism II :
$\begin{gathered}
\mathrm{O}_3 \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{O}_2+\mathrm{O} \text { Fast } \\
\mathrm{NO}_2+\mathrm{O} \rightarrow \mathrm{NO}_3 \text { Slow } \\
\mathrm{NO}_3+\mathrm{NO}_2 \rightarrow \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5 \text { Fast }
\end{gathered}$

Which of the following are correct?
options: Mechanism I is consistent with experimental observation
Mechanism II is consistent with experimental observation
Mechanism I is not consistent with experimental observation
Mechanism II is not consistent with experimental observation
questions.solution:
Mechanism I
$\text { rate }=\mathrm{K}\left[\mathrm{NO}_2\right]^1\left[\mathrm{O}_3\right]^1$

Mechanism II
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { rate }=\mathrm{K}\left[\mathrm{NO}_2\right][\mathrm{O}] \\
& =\mathrm{K}\left[\mathrm{NO}_2\right] \cdot \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{eq}} \frac{\left[\mathrm{O}_3\right]}{\left[\mathrm{O}_2\right]} \\
& \mathrm{K}\left(\mathrm{~K}_{\mathrm{eq}}\right) \frac{\left[\mathrm{NO}_2\right]\left[\mathrm{O}_3\right]}{\left[\mathrm{O}_2\right]}
\end{aligned}$

Hence, Mechanism II is not consistent with observation
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Select the correct statement (s) among the following
options: Increase in concentration of reactant increases the rate of zero order reaction
Rate constant $K$ is equal to collision frequency $A$, if $E_a=0$
Rate constant $K$ is equal to collision frequency $A$ if $E_a=\infty$
questions.solution:
Rate of zero order reactions is independent of the concentration of reaction
In this equation $\mathrm{K}=\mathrm{Ae}^{-\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{a}} / \mathrm{RT}}$
$K=A$ when $E_a=0$
$\log K=\log A-\frac{E_a}{2.303 R T}$
Plot of $\log K$ against $1 / T$ is straight line with negative slope.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

Number of peaks represent, the transitions state
Rate of reaction dene upon the activation energy of the reaction.
